items.correct: The 'blowing out' of the fires of craving, hatred and delusion — liberation from the cycle of rebirth (samsara), not a heavenly afterlife.
items.misconception: Nirvana is the Buddhist version of heaven.
items.random_wrong: Nirvana is the name of a Buddhist god.
items.why: Students map nirvana onto Christian heaven — a place you go after dying. Nirvana is the cessation of the conditions that cause rebirth; it is more an end than a destination.

items.correct: The first five books of the Hebrew Bible (Genesis, Exodus, Leviticus, Numbers, Deuteronomy) — traditionally attributed to Moses and containing law, narrative and covenant.
items.misconception: The Torah is the same as the Book of Genesis.
items.random_wrong: The Torah is a single scroll containing every Jewish text.
items.why: The Torah is five books, not one. Wider Jewish scripture (Tanakh) also includes Nevi'im (Prophets) and Ketuvim (Writings). Students often equate Torah with Genesis or with the whole Hebrew Bible.

items.correct: The absolute oneness and indivisibility of Allah — the most fundamental Islamic belief. Its opposite, shirk (associating partners with God), is the gravest sin.
items.misconception: Tawhid is the Islamic word for the Trinity.
items.random_wrong: Tawhid is the name of an Islamic prayer position.
items.why: Tawhid is the strict denial of any plurality in God — directly contrasting with Trinitarian Christianity. Students sometimes invert the meaning entirely. The Quran's emphasis on Tawhid shapes Islamic theology, art (no images) and ethics.
Correct answer: The declaration of faith. “The Hajj pilgrimage” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: What is the washing before prayer called?
items.why: Correct answer: Wudu. “Hajj” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: How many times a day do Muslims perform Salah?
items.why: The real figure is 5. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
